simple_1.question: What is the highest eligible free rate for K-12 students in the schools in Alameda County?
simple_1.evidence: Eligible free rate for K-12 = `Free Meal Count (K-12)` / `Enrollment (K-12)`
simple_1.SQL: SELECT `Free Meal Count (K-12)` / `Enrollment (K-12)` FROM frpm WHERE `County Name` = 'Alameda' ORDER BY (CAST(`Free Meal Count (K-12)` AS REAL) / `Enrollment (K-12)`) DESC LIMIT 1

simple_2.question: Please list the zip code of all the charter schools in Fresno County Office of Education.
simple_2.evidence: Charter schools refers to `Charter School (Y/N)` = 1 in the table fprm
simple_2.SQL: SELECT T2.Zip FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T1.`District Name` = 'Fresno County Office of Education' AND T1.`Charter School (Y/N)` = 1

simple_3.question: What is the unabbreviated mailing street address of the school with the highest FRPM count for K-12 students?
simple_3.SQL: SELECT T2.MailStreet FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode ORDER BY T1.`FRPM Count (K-12)` DESC LIMIT 1

simple_4.question: How many schools with an average score in Math greater than 400 in the SAT test are exclusively virtual?
simple_4.SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.School) FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.Virtual = 'F' AND T1.AvgScrMath > 400

simple_5.question: Among the schools with the SAT test takers of over 500, please list the schools that are magnet schools or offer a magnet program.
simple_5.evidence: Magnet schools or offer a magnet program means that Magnet = 1
simple_5.SQL: SELECT T2.School FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.Magnet = 1 AND T1.NumTstTakr > 500

simple_6.question: What is the phone number of the school that has the highest number of test takers with an SAT score of over 1500?
simple_6.SQL: SELECT T2.Phone FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode ORDER BY T1.NumGE1500 DESC LIMIT 1

simple_7.question: What is the number of SAT test takers of the schools with the highest FRPM count for K-12 students?
simple_7.SQL: SELECT NumTstTakr FROM satscores WHERE cds = ( SELECT CDSCode FROM frpm ORDER BY `FRPM Count (K-12)` DESC LIMIT 1 )

simple_8.question: Among the schools with the average score in Math over 560 in the SAT test, how many schools are directly charter-funded?
simple_8.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.`School Code`) FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE T1.AvgScrMath > 560 AND T2.`Charter Funding Type` = 'Directly funded'

simple_9.question: For the school with the highest average score in Reading in the SAT test, what is its FRPM count for students aged 5-17?
simple_9.SQL: SELECT T2.`FRPM Count (Ages 5-17)` FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode ORDER BY T1.AvgScrRead DESC LIMIT 1

simple_10.question: Please list the codes of the schools with a total enrollment of over 500.
simple_10.evidence: Total enrollment can be represented by `Enrollment (K-12)` + `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`
simple_10.SQL: SELECT T2.CDSCode FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.`Enrollment (K-12)` + T2.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` > 500

simple_11.question: Please list the phone numbers of the schools with the top 3 SAT excellence rate.
simple_11.SQL: SELECT T1.Phone FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds ORDER BY CAST(T2.NumGE1500 AS REAL) / T2.NumTstTakr DESC LIMIT 3

simple_12.question: List the top five schools, by descending order, from the highest to the lowest, the most number of Enrollment (Ages 5-17). Please give their NCES school identification number.
simple_12.SQL: SELECT T1.NCESSchool FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode ORDER BY T2.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` DESC LIMIT 5

simple_13.question: Which active district has the highest average score in Reading?
simple_13.SQL: SELECT T1.District FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T1.StatusType = 'Active' ORDER BY T2.AvgScrRead DESC LIMIT 1

simple_14.question: How many schools in merged Alameda have number of test takers less than 100?
simple_14.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.CDSCode) FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T1.StatusType = 'Merged' AND T2.NumTstTakr < 100 AND T1.County = 'Lake'

simple_15.question: How many gas stations in CZE has Premium gas?
simple_15.SQL: SELECT COUNT(GasStationID) FROM gasstations WHERE Country = 'CZE' AND Segment = 'Premium'

simple_16.question: What is the ratio of customers who pay in EUR against customers who pay in CZK?
simple_16.evidence: ratio of customers who pay in EUR against customers who pay in CZK = count(Currency = 'EUR') / count(Currency = 'CZK').
simple_16.SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(Currency = 'EUR', 1, 0)) AS FLOAT) / SUM(IIF(Currency = 'CZK', 1, 0)) AS ratio FROM customers

simple_17.question: Which year recorded the most gas use paid in EUR?
simple_17.SQL: SELECT SUBSTRING(T2.Date, 1, 4) FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T1.Currency = 'EUR' GROUP BY SUBSTRING(T2.Date, 1, 4) ORDER BY SUM(T2.Consumption) DESC LIMIT 1

simple_18.question: Which segment had the least consumption?
simple_18.SQL: SELECT T1.Segment FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID GROUP BY T1.Segment ORDER BY SUM(T2.Consumption) ASC LIMIT 1

simple_19.question: How much did customer 6 consume in total between August and November 2013?
simple_19.evidence: Between August And November 2013 refers to Between 201308 And 201311; The first 4 strings of the Date values in the yearmonth table can represent year; The 5th and 6th string of the date can refer to month.
simple_19.SQL: SELECT SUM(Consumption) FROM yearmonth WHERE CustomerID = 6 AND Date BETWEEN '201308' AND '201311'

simple_20.question: How many more "discount" gas stations does the Czech Republic have compared to Slovakia?
simple_20.evidence: Czech Republic can be represented as the Country value in gasstations table is 'CZE'; Slovakia can be represented as the Country value in the gasstations table is 'SVK';  Computation of more "discount" gas stations= Total no. of discount gas stations in Czech Republic - Total no. of discount gas stations in Slovakia
simple_20.SQL: SELECT SUM(IIF(Country = 'CZE', 1, 0)) - SUM(IIF(Country = 'SVK', 1, 0)) FROM gasstations WHERE Segment = 'Discount'

simple_21.question: How much more was customer 7 consuming in April 2013 than customer 5?
simple_21.evidence: April 2013 refers to 201304 in the yearmonth.date
simple_21.SQL: SELECT SUM(IIF(CustomerID = 7, Consumption, 0)) - SUM(IIF(CustomerID = 5, Consumption, 0)) FROM yearmonth WHERE Date = '201304'

simple_22.question: Is it true that more SMEs pay in Czech koruna than in euros? If so, how many more?
simple_22.evidence: Amount of more SMEs = Total of SMEs pay using Currency CZK - Total of SMEs pay using Currency EUR
simple_22.SQL: SELECT SUM(Currency = 'CZK') - SUM(Currency = 'EUR') FROM customers WHERE Segment = 'SME'

simple_23.question: Who among KAM's customers consumed the most? How much did it consume?
simple_23.SQL: SELECT T2.CustomerID, SUM(T2.Consumption) FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T1.Segment = 'KAM' GROUP BY T2.CustomerID ORDER BY SUM(T2.Consumption) DESC LIMIT 1

simple_24.question: How much did the KAM customers consume in total in May 2013?
simple_24.SQL: SELECT SUM(T2.Consumption) FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T2.Date = '201305' AND T1.Segment = 'KAM'

simple_25.question: Which country has more "value for money" gas stations? Please give a total number of "value for money" gas stations in each country.
simple_25.SQL: SELECT Country , ( SELECT COUNT(GasStationID) FROM gasstations WHERE Segment = 'Value for money' ) FROM gasstations WHERE Segment = 'Value for money' GROUP BY Country ORDER BY COUNT(GasStationID) DESC LIMIT 1

simple_26.question: How many accounts who have region in Prague are eligible for loans?
simple_26.evidence: A3 contains the data of region
simple_26.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.account_id) FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN district AS T3 ON T1.district_id = T3.district_id WHERE T3.A3 = 'Prague'

simple_27.question: The average unemployment ratio of 1995 and 1996, which one has higher percentage?
simple_27.evidence: A12 refers to unemploymant rate 1995; A13 refers to unemploymant rate 1996
simple_27.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT IIF(AVG(A13) > AVG(A12), '1996', '1995') FROM district

simple_28.question: List out the no. of districts that have female average salary is more than 6000 but less than 10000?
simple_28.evidence: A11 refers to average salary; Female mapps to gender = 'F'
simple_28.SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.district_id)  FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T1.gender = 'F' AND T2.A11 BETWEEN 6000 AND 10000

simple_29.question: How many customers who choose statement of weekly issuance are Owner?
simple_29.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.account_id) FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE T2.type = 'OWNER' AND T1.frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE'

simple_30.question: List out the id number of client who choose statement of issuance after transaction are Disponent?
simple_30.evidence: 'POPLATEK PO OBRATU' stands for issuance after transaction
simple_30.SQL: SELECT T2.client_id FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE T1.frequency = 'POPLATEK PO OBRATU' AND T2.type = 'DISPONENT'

simple_31.question: List out the accounts who have the earliest trading date in 1995 ?
simple_31.SQL: SELECT account_id FROM trans WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', date) = '1995' ORDER BY date ASC LIMIT 1

simple_32.question: State different accounts who have account opening date before 1997 and own an amount of money greater than 3000USD
simple_32.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.account_id FROM trans AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T2.date) < '1997' AND T1.amount > 3000

simple_33.question: Which client issued his/her card in 1994/3/3, give his/her client id.
simple_33.SQL: SELECT T2.client_id FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.client_id INNER JOIN card AS T3 ON T2.disp_id = T3.disp_id WHERE T3.issued = '1994-03-03'

simple_34.question: The transaction of 840 USD happened in 1998/10/14, when was this account opened?
simple_34.SQL: SELECT T1.date FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN trans AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE T2.amount = 840 AND T2.date = '1998-10-14'

simple_35.question: There was a loan approved in 1994/8/25, where was that account opened, give the district Id of the branch.
simple_35.SQL: SELECT T1.district_id FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE T2.date = '1994-08-25'

simple_36.question: What is the biggest amount of transaction that the client whose card was opened in 1996/10/21 made?
simple_36.SQL: SELECT T4.amount FROM card AS T1 JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.disp_id = T2.disp_id JOIN account AS T3 on T2.account_id = T3.account_id JOIN trans AS T4 on T3.account_id = T4.account_id WHERE T1.issued = '1996-10-21' ORDER BY T4.amount DESC LIMIT 1

simple_37.question: What is the gender of the oldest client who opened his/her account in the highest average salary branch?
simple_37.evidence: Earlier birthdate refers to older age; A11 refers to average salary
simple_37.SQL: SELECT T2.gender FROM district AS T1 INNER JOIN client AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id ORDER BY T1.A11 DESC, T2.birth_date ASC LIMIT 1

simple_38.question: For the client who applied the biggest loan, what was his/her first amount of transaction after opened the account?
simple_38.SQL: SELECT T3.amount FROM loan AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN trans AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id ORDER BY T1.amount DESC, T3.date ASC LIMIT 1

simple_39.question: How many clients opened their accounts in Jesenik branch were women?
simple_39.evidence: A2 has region names; Woman and female share the same meaning; female refers to gender = 'F'
simple_39.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.client_id) FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T1.gender = 'F' AND T2.A2 = 'Jesenik'

simple_40.question: What is the disposition id of the client who made 5100 USD transaction in 1998/9/2?
simple_40.SQL: SELECT T1.disp_id FROM disp AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN trans AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T3.date='1997-08-20' AND T3.amount = 5100

simple_41.question: What is the surname of the driver with the best lap time in race number 19 in the second qualifying period?
simple_41.evidence: race number refers to raceId; second qualifying period refers to q2; best lap time refers to MIN(q2);
simple_41.SQL: SELECT T2.surname FROM qualifying AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T1.raceId = 19 ORDER BY T1.q2 ASC LIMIT 1

simple_42.question: Please list the year during which the race is held on circuits in Shanghai.
simple_42.evidence: Shanghai is a name of location;
simple_42.SQL: SELECT T2.year FROM circuits AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T2.circuitID = T1.circuitId WHERE T1.location = 'Shanghai'

simple_43.question: Where can the introduction of the races held on Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya be found?
simple_43.evidence: introduction of races refers to url; Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya is a name of circuit;
simple_43.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.url FROM circuits AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T2.circuitID = T1.circuitId WHERE T1.name = 'Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya'

simple_44.question: Please give the name of the race held on the circuits in Germany.
simple_44.evidence: Germany is a name of country;
simple_44.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name FROM circuits AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T2.circuitID = T1.circuitId WHERE T1.country = 'Germany'

simple_45.question: Please list the positions of the circuits built by the constructor Renault.
simple_45.evidence: Renault is a name of constructor;
simple_45.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.position FROM constructorStandings AS T1 INNER JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T2.constructorId = T1.constructorId WHERE T2.name = 'Renault'

simple_46.question: Please give the names of the races held on the circuits in Spain.
simple_46.evidence: Spain is a name of country;
simple_46.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name FROM circuits AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T2.circuitID = T1.circuitId WHERE T1.country = 'Spain'

simple_47.question: What is the coordinates location of the circuits for Australian grand prix?
simple_47.evidence: coordinate position/location refers to lat, lng; circuits for Australian grand prix refers to races.name = 'Australian Grand Prix'
simple_47.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.lat, T1.lng FROM circuits AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T2.circuitID = T1.circuitId WHERE T2.name = 'Australian Grand Prix'

simple_48.question: Where can I find the information about the races held on Sepang International Circuit?
simple_48.evidence: information about races refers to url;
simple_48.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.url FROM circuits AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T2.circuitID = T1.circuitId WHERE T1.name = 'Sepang International Circuit'

simple_49.question: Please list the time of the races held on Sepang International Circuit.
simple_49.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.time FROM circuits AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T2.circuitID = T1.circuitId WHERE T1.name = 'Sepang International Circuit'

simple_50.question: Give the coordinate position for Abu Dhabi Grand Prix.
simple_50.evidence: coordinate position/location refers to lat, lng; Abu Dhabi Grand Prix refers to races.name = 'Abu Dhabi Grand Prix'
simple_50.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.lat, T1.lng FROM circuits AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T2.circuitID = T1.circuitId WHERE T2.name = 'Abu Dhabi Grand Prix'

simple_51.question: Which country is the constructor which got 1 point in the race No. 24 from?
simple_51.SQL: SELECT T2.nationality FROM constructorResults AS T1 INNER JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T2.constructorId = T1.constructorId WHERE T1.raceId = 24 AND T1.points = 1

simple_52.question: What's Bruno Senna's Q1 result in the qualifying race No. 354?
simple_52.evidence: race number refers to raceId; Bruno Senna refers to the full name of the driver; Full name of the driver refers to drivers.forename and drivers.surname;
simple_52.SQL: SELECT T1.q1 FROM qualifying AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T1.raceId = 354 AND T2.forename = 'Bruno' AND T2.surname = 'Senna'

simple_53.question: For the driver who had the Q2 time as 0:01:40 in the qualifying race No. 355, what is his nationality?
simple_53.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.nationality FROM qualifying AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T1.raceId = 355 AND T1.q2 LIKE '1:40%'

simple_54.question: What is his number of the driver who finished 0:01:54 in the Q3 of qualifying race No.903?
simple_54.evidence: race number refers to raceId; finished 0:0M:SS in the Q3 refers to q3 LIKE 'M:SS%'
simple_54.SQL: SELECT T2.number FROM qualifying AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T1.raceId = 903 AND T1.q3 LIKE '1:54%'

simple_55.question: For the Bahrain Grand Prix in 2007, how many drivers not finished the game?
simple_55.evidence: Bahrain Grand Prix refers to races.name = 'Bahrain Grand Prix'; drivers who finished the race refers to time is not empty (i.e. time IS NOT NULL);
simple_55.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T3.driverId) FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN results AS T2 ON T2.raceId = T1.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T3.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T1.year = 2007 AND T1.name = 'Bahrain Grand Prix' AND T2.time IS NULL

simple_56.question: Show me the season page of year when the race No. 901 took place.
simple_56.SQL: SELECT T2.url FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN seasons AS T2 ON T2.year = T1.year WHERE T1.raceId = 901

simple_57.question: For the race happened on 2015/11/29, how many drivers finished the game?
simple_57.evidence: game and race are synonyms; drivers who finished the race should have record in time;
simple_57.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.driverId) FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN results AS T2 ON T2.raceId = T1.raceId WHERE T1.date = '2015-11-29' AND T2.time IS NOT NULL

simple_58.question: For the driver who set the fastest lap speed in race No.933, where does he come from?
simple_58.evidence: fastest lap speed refers to MAX(fastestLapSpeed);
simple_58.SQL: SELECT T1.nationality FROM drivers AS T1 INNER JOIN results AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T2.raceId = 933 AND T2.fastestLapTime IS NOT NULL ORDER BY T2.fastestLapSpeed DESC LIMIT 1

simple_59.question: Where is Malaysian Grand Prix held? Give the location coordinates.
simple_59.evidence: location coordinates refers to (lat, lng); Malaysian Grand Prix refers to races.name = 'Malaysian Grand Prix'
simple_59.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.lat, T1.lng FROM circuits AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T2.circuitID = T1.circuitId WHERE T2.name = 'Malaysian Grand Prix'

simple_60.question: What's Lucas di Grassi's Q1 result in the race No. 345?
simple_60.SQL: SELECT T1.q1 FROM qualifying AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T1.raceId = 345 AND T2.forename = 'Lucas' AND T2.surname = 'di Grassi'

simple_61.question: For the driver who had the Q2 time as 0:01:15 in race No. 347, where is he from?
simple_61.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.nationality FROM qualifying AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T1.raceId = 347 AND T1.q2 LIKE '1:15%'

simple_62.question: In the race No. 45, for the driver who had the Q3 time as 0:01:33, what is his abbreviated code?
simple_62.evidence: race number refers to raceId; had the Q3 time as 0:0M:SS refers to q3 LIKE 'M:SS%'
simple_62.SQL: SELECT T2.code FROM qualifying AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T1.raceId = 45 AND T1.q3 LIKE '1:33%'

simple_63.question: What is the actual finish time for Bruce McLaren in the race No.743?
simple_63.SQL: SELECT T2.time FROM drivers AS T1 INNER JOIN results AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T2.raceId = 743 AND T1.forename = 'Bruce' AND T1.surname = 'McLaren'

simple_64.question: Who finished second in the San Marino Grand Prix in 2006?
simple_64.SQL: SELECT T3.forename, T3.surname FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN results AS T2 ON T2.raceId = T1.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T3.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T1.year = 2006 AND T1.name = 'San Marino Grand Prix' AND T2.position = 2

simple_65.question: Show me the season page of year when the race No. 901 took place.
simple_65.evidence: the season page refers to url; race number refers to raceId;
simple_65.SQL: SELECT T2.url FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN seasons AS T2 ON T2.year = T1.year WHERE T1.raceId = 901

simple_66.question: For the race happened in 2015/11/29, how many drivers did not finish the game?
simple_66.evidence: game and race are synonyms; drivers who didn't finish the race should have record in time;
simple_66.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.driverId) FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN results AS T2 ON T2.raceId = T1.raceId WHERE T1.date = '2015-11-29' AND T2.time IS NULL

simple_67.question: Who was the driver that got the best lap time in the race No. 348? Give his full name.
simple_67.evidence: race number refers to raceId; the best lap time refers to MIN(time)
simple_67.SQL: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM lapTimes AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T1.raceId = 348 ORDER BY T1.time ASC LIMIT 1

simple_68.question: Which year was the first Singapore Grand Prix?
simple_68.evidence: the first race refers to race happened in min(year);
simple_68.SQL: SELECT year FROM races WHERE name = 'Singapore Grand Prix' ORDER BY year ASC LIMIT 1

simple_69.question: How many races were there in 2005? Name all the races in descending order.
simple_69.SQL: SELECT name FROM races WHERE year = 2005 ORDER BY name DESC

simple_70.question: What is the most common bond type?
simple_70.evidence: most common bond type refers MAX(COUNT(bond_type))
simple_70.SQL: SELECT T.bond_type FROM ( SELECT bond_type, COUNT(bond_id) FROM bond GROUP BY bond_type ORDER BY COUNT(bond_id) DESC LIMIT 1 ) AS T

simple_71.question: In the non-carcinogenic molecules, how many contain chlorine atoms?
simple_71.evidence: non-carcinogenic molecules refers to label = '-'; chlorine atoms refers to element = 'cl'
simple_71.SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.molecule_id) FROM molecule AS T1 INNER JOIN atom AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T2.element = 'cl' AND T1.label = '-'

simple_72.question: In the molecule containing sodium atoms, how many are non-carcinogenic?
simple_72.evidence: non-carcinogenic refers to label = '-'; sodium atoms refers to element = 'na'
simple_72.SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.molecule_id) FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T1.element = 'na' AND T2.label = '-'

simple_73.question: Find the triple-bonded molecules which are carcinogenic.
simple_73.evidence: triple-bonded molecules refers to bond_type = '#'; carcinogenic refers to label = '+'
simple_73.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.molecule_id FROM bond AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T1.bond_type = '#' AND T2.label = '+'

simple_74.question: How many triple type bonds are there?
simple_74.evidence: triple type bonds refers to bond_type = '#'
simple_74.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T.bond_id) FROM bond AS T WHERE T.bond_type = '#'

simple_75.question: In how many atoms is there no bromine?
simple_75.evidence: atoms with no bromine refers to element ! = 'br'
simple_75.SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T.atom_id) FROM atom AS T WHERE T.element <> 'br'

simple_76.question: Of the first 100 molecules in number order, how many are carcinogenic?
simple_76.evidence: first 100 molecules in number order refers to molecule_id between 'TR000' and 'TR099'; label = '+' means molecules are carcinogenic
simple_76.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T.molecule_id) FROM molecule AS T WHERE molecule_id BETWEEN 'TR000' AND 'TR099' AND T.label = '+'

simple_77.question: Identify by their ID the molecules in which there is carbon.
simple_77.SQL: SELECT T.molecule_id FROM atom AS T WHERE T.element = 'c'

simple_78.question: Chlorine is in what type of bond?
simple_78.evidence: type of bond refers to bond_type; chlorine refers to element = 'cl'
simple_78.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.bond_type FROM bond AS T1 INNER JOIN connected AS T2 ON T1.bond_id = T2.bond_id INNER JOIN atom AS T3 ON T2.atom_id = T3.atom_id WHERE T3.element = 'cl'

simple_79.question: What atoms are connected in single type bonds?
simple_79.evidence: single type bond refers to bond_type = '-';
simple_79.SQL: SELECT T2.atom_id, T2.atom_id2 FROM bond AS T1 INNER JOIN connected AS T2 ON T1.bond_id = T2.bond_id WHERE T1.bond_type = '-'

simple_80.question: Indicate which atoms are connected in non-carcinogenic type molecules.
simple_80.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.atom_id FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id INNER JOIN connected AS T3 ON T1.atom_id = T3.atom_id WHERE T2.label = '-'

simple_81.question: What type of label is not on molecules with atoms with tin?
simple_81.evidence: tin refers to element ! = 'sn'; label = '+' mean molecules are carcinogenic; label = '-' means molecules are non-carcinogenic
simple_81.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.label FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T1.element != 'sn'

simple_82.question: Identify all connected atoms with a triple bond.
simple_82.SQL: SELECT T2.atom_id, T2.atom_id2 FROM bond AS T1 INNER JOIN connected AS T2 ON T1.bond_id = T2.bond_id WHERE T1.bond_type = '#'

simple_83.question: Identify all the atoms that are connected to the atoms of the TR181 molecule.
simple_83.SQL: SELECT T2.atom_id, T2.atom_id2 FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN connected AS T2 ON T2.atom_id = T1.atom_id WHERE T1.molecule_id = 'TR181'

simple_84.question: What are the atoms that are bonded in the molecule TR001 with the bond ID of TR001_2_6?
simple_84.evidence: TR001 is the molecule id; TR001_2_6 is the bond id
simple_84.SQL: SELECT SUBSTR(T.bond_id, 1, 7) AS atom_id1 , T.molecule_id || SUBSTR(T.bond_id, 8, 2) AS atom_id2 FROM bond AS T WHERE T.molecule_id = 'TR001' AND T.bond_id = 'TR001_2_6'

simple_85.question: What are the atom IDs of the bond TR000_2_5?
simple_85.SQL: SELECT T.atom_id FROM connected AS T WHERE T.bond_id = 'TR000_2_5'

moderate_1.question: Please list the lowest three eligible free rates for students aged 5-17 in continuation schools.
moderate_1.evidence: Eligible free rates for students aged 5-17 = `Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`
moderate_1.SQL: SELECT `Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` FROM frpm WHERE `Educational Option Type` = 'Continuation School' AND `Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` IS NOT NULL ORDER BY `Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` ASC LIMIT 3

moderate_2.question: Please list the phone numbers of the direct charter-funded schools that are opened after 2000/1/1.
moderate_2.evidence: Charter schools refers to `Charter School (Y/N)` = 1 in the frpm
moderate_2.SQL: SELECT T2.Phone FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T1.`Charter Funding Type` = 'Directly funded' AND T1.`Charter School (Y/N)` = 1 AND T2.OpenDate > '2000-01-01'

moderate_3.question: Among the schools with an SAT excellence rate of over 0.3, what is the highest eligible free rate for students aged 5-17?
moderate_3.evidence: Excellence rate = NumGE1500 / NumTstTakr; Eligible free rates for students aged 5-17 = `Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`
moderate_3.SQL: SELECT MAX(CAST(T1.`Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` AS REAL) / T1.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE CAST(T2.NumGE1500 AS REAL) / T2.NumTstTakr > 0.3

moderate_4.question: List the names of schools with more than 30 difference in enrollements between K-12 and ages 5-17? Please also give the full street adress of the schools.
moderate_4.SQL: SELECT T1.School, T1.Street FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.`Enrollment (K-12)` - T2.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` > 30

moderate_5.question: Give the names of the schools with the percent eligible for free meals in K-12 is more than 0.1 and test takers whose test score is greater than or equal to 1500?
moderate_5.evidence: Percent eligible for free meals = Free Meal Count (K-12) / Total (Enrollment (K-12)
moderate_5.SQL: SELECT T2.`School Name` FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE CAST(T2.`Free Meal Count (K-12)` AS REAL) / T2.`Enrollment (K-12)` > 0.1 AND T1.NumGE1500 > 0

moderate_6.question: Name schools in Riverside which the average of average math score for SAT is grater than 400, what is the funding type of these schools?
moderate_6.evidence: Average of average math = sum(average math scores) / count(schools).
moderate_6.SQL: SELECT T1.sname, T2.`Charter Funding Type` FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.`District Name` LIKE 'Riverside%' GROUP BY T1.sname, T2.`Charter Funding Type` HAVING CAST(SUM(T1.AvgScrMath) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.cds) > 400

moderate_7.question: State the names and full communication address of high schools in Monterey which has more than 800 free or reduced price meals for ages 15-17?
moderate_7.evidence: Full communication address should include Street, City, State and zip code if any.
moderate_7.SQL: SELECT T1.`School Name`, T2.Street, T2.City, T2.State, T2.Zip FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.County = 'Monterey' AND T1.`Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` > 800 AND T1.`School Type` = 'High Schools (Public)'

moderate_8.question: What is the average score in writing for the schools that were opened after 1991 or closed before 2000? List the school names along with the score. Also, list the communication number of the schools if there is any.
moderate_8.evidence: Communication number refers to phone number.
moderate_8.SQL: SELECT T2.School, T1.AvgScrWrite, T2.Phone FROM schools AS T2 LEFT JOIN satscores AS T1 ON T2.CDSCode = T1.cds WHERE strftime('%Y', T2.OpenDate) > '1991' OR strftime('%Y', T2.ClosedDate) < '2000'

moderate_9.question: What is the eligible free rate of the 10th and 11th schools with the highest enrolment for students in grades 1 through 12?
moderate_9.evidence: K-12 refers to students in grades 1 through 12; Eligible free rate for K-12 = `Free Meal Count (K-12)` / `Enrollment (K-12)`
moderate_9.SQL: SELECT CAST(`Free Meal Count (K-12)` AS REAL) / `Enrollment (K-12)` FROM frpm ORDER BY `Enrollment (K-12)` DESC LIMIT 9, 2

moderate_10.question: What is the eligible free or reduced price meal rate for the top 5 schools in grades 1-12 with the highest free or reduced price meal count of the schools with the ownership code 66?
moderate_10.evidence: grades 1-12 means K-12; Eligible free or reduced price meal rate for K-12 = `FRPM Count (K-12)` / `Enrollment (K-12)`
moderate_10.SQL: SELECT CAST(T1.`FRPM Count (K-12)` AS REAL) / T1.`Enrollment (K-12)` FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.SOC = 66 ORDER BY T1.`FRPM Count (K-12)` DESC LIMIT 5

moderate_11.question: If there are any, what are the websites address of the schools with a free meal count of 1,900-2,000 to students aged 5-17? Include the name of the school.
moderate_11.SQL: SELECT T2.Website, T1.`School Name` FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T1.`Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` BETWEEN 1900 AND 2000 AND T2.Website IS NOT NULL

moderate_12.question: What is the free rate for students between the ages of 5 and 17 at the school run by Kacey Gibson?
moderate_12.evidence: Eligible free rates for students aged 5-17 = `Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`
moderate_12.SQL: SELECT CAST(T2.`Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` AS REAL) / T2.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T1.AdmFName1 = 'Kacey' AND T1.AdmLName1 = 'Gibson'

moderate_13.question: In 2012, who had the least consumption in LAM?
moderate_13.evidence: Year 2012 can be presented as Between 201201 And 201212; The first 4 strings of the Date values in the yearmonth table can represent year.
moderate_13.SQL: SELECT T1.CustomerID FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T1.Segment = 'LAM' AND SUBSTR(T2.Date, 1, 4) = '2012' GROUP BY T1.CustomerID ORDER BY SUM(T2.Consumption) ASC LIMIT 1

moderate_14.question: What was the average monthly consumption of customers in SME for the year 2013?
moderate_14.evidence: Average Monthly consumption = AVG(Consumption) / 12; Year 2013 can be presented as Between 201301 And 201312; The first 4 strings of the Date values in the yearmonth table can represent year.
moderate_14.SQL: SELECT AVG(T2.Consumption) / 12 FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE SUBSTR(T2.Date, 1, 4) = '2013' AND T1.Segment = 'SME'

moderate_15.question: Which customers, paying in CZK, consumed the most gas in 2011?
moderate_15.evidence: Year 2011 can be presented as Between 201101 And 201112, which means between January and December in 2011
moderate_15.SQL: SELECT T1.CustomerID FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T1.Currency = 'CZK' AND T2.Date BETWEEN 201101 AND 201112 GROUP BY T1.CustomerID ORDER BY SUM(T2.Consumption) DESC LIMIT 1

moderate_16.question: How many customers in KAM had a consumption of less than 30,000 for the year 2012?
moderate_16.evidence: Year 2012 can be presented as Between 201201 And 201212, which means between January and December in 2012
moderate_16.SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ( SELECT T2.CustomerID FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T1.Segment = 'KAM' AND SUBSTRING(T2.Date, 1, 4) = '2012' GROUP BY T2.CustomerID HAVING SUM(T2.Consumption) < 30000 ) AS t1

moderate_17.question: Which year recorded the most consumption of gas paid in CZK?
moderate_17.evidence: The first 4 strings of the Date values in the yearmonth table can represent year.
moderate_17.SQL: SELECT SUBSTR(T2.Date, 1, 4) FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T1.Currency = 'CZK' GROUP BY SUBSTR(T2.Date, 1, 4) ORDER BY SUM(T2.Consumption) DESC LIMIT 1

moderate_18.question: What was the gas consumption peak month for SME customers in 2013?
moderate_18.evidence: Year 2013 can be presented as Between 201301 And 201312; The first 4 strings of the Date values in the yearmonth table can represent year; The 5th and 6th string of the date can refer to month.
moderate_18.SQL: SELECT SUBSTR(T2.Date, 5, 2) FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE SUBSTR(T2.Date, 1, 4) = '2013' AND T1.Segment = 'SME' GROUP BY SUBSTR(T2.Date, 5, 2) ORDER BY SUM(T2.Consumption) DESC LIMIT 1

moderate_19.question: Which LAM customer used the Euro as their currency and had the highest consumption in October 2013?
moderate_19.evidence: October 2013 refers to 201310 in the yearmonth.date
moderate_19.SQL: SELECT T1.CustomerID FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T1.Segment = 'LAM' AND T2.Date = '201310' AND T1.Currency = 'EUR' GROUP BY T1.CustomerID ORDER BY SUM(T2.Consumption) DESC LIMIT 1

moderate_20.question: How many accounts who choose issuance after transaction are staying in East Bohemia region?
moderate_20.evidence: A3 contains the data of region; 'POPLATEK PO OBRATU' represents for 'issuance after transaction'.
moderate_20.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.account_id) FROM district AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T1.A3 = 'east Bohemia' AND T2.frequency = 'POPLATEK PO OBRATU'

moderate_21.question: How many male customers who are living in North Bohemia have average salary greater than 8000?
moderate_21.evidence: Male means that gender = 'M'; A3 refers to region; A11 pertains to average salary.
moderate_21.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.client_id) FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T1.gender = 'M' AND T2.A3 = 'north Bohemia' AND T2.A11 > 8000

moderate_22.question: List out the account numbers of clients who are youngest and have highest average salary?
moderate_22.evidence: If the person A's birthdate < B's birthdate, it means that person B is younger than person A; A11 refers to average salary
moderate_22.SQL: SELECT T1.account_id  FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN client AS T3 ON T2.client_id = T3.client_id INNER JOIN district AS T4 on T4.district_id = T1.district_id WHERE T2.client_id = ( SELECT client_id FROM client ORDER BY birth_date DESC LIMIT 1) GROUP BY T4.A11, T1.account_id

moderate_23.question: Among the accounts who have approved loan date in 1997, list out the accounts that have the lowest approved amount and choose weekly issuance statement.
moderate_23.SQL: SELECT T2.account_id FROM loan AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T1.date) = '1997' AND T2.frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE' ORDER BY T1.amount LIMIT 1

moderate_24.question: Among the accounts who have loan validity more than 12 months, list out the accounts that have the highest approved amount and have account opening date in 1993.
moderate_24.evidence: Loan validity more than 12 months refers to duration > 12
moderate_24.SQL: SELECT T1.account_id FROM loan AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T2.date) = '1993' AND T1.duration > 12 ORDER BY T1.amount DESC LIMIT 1

moderate_25.question: Among the account opened, how many female customers who were born before 1950 and stayed in Sokolov?
moderate_25.evidence: Customers refer to clients; Female refers to gender = 'F'; Names of districts appear in column A2
moderate_25.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.client_id) FROM district AS T1 INNER JOIN client AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T2.gender = 'F' AND STRFTIME('%Y', T2.birth_date) < '1950' AND T1.A2 = 'Sokolov'

moderate_26.question: What is the percentage of loan amount that has been fully paid with no issue.
moderate_26.evidence: Loan paid with no issue means contract finished, no problems; status = 'A' means contract finished, no problems; Percentage of accounts by condition = [(total(amount) & condition) / (total amount)] * 100%
moderate_26.SQL: SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'A' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100) / SUM(amount) FROM loan

moderate_27.question: For loan amount less than USD100,000, what is the percentage of accounts that is still running with no issue.
moderate_27.evidence: Status = 'C' stands for running contract, ok so far; Percentage of accounts by condition = [(total(amount) & condition) / (total amount)] * 100.
moderate_27.SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(status = 'C') AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(account_id) FROM loan WHERE amount < 100000

moderate_28.question: For accounts in 1993 with statement issued after transaction, list the account ID, district name and district region.
moderate_28.evidence: Records about district names could be found in A2; A3 contains the information about regions. 'POPLATEK PO OBRATU' stands for issuance after transaction
moderate_28.SQL: SELECT T1.account_id, T2.A2, T2.A3 FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T1.frequency = 'POPLATEK PO OBRATU' AND STRFTIME('%Y', T1.date)= '1993'

moderate_29.question: From Year 1995 to 2000, who are the accounts holders from 'east Bohemia'. State the account ID the frequency of statement issuance.
moderate_29.evidence: Accounts holder refers to the person who own this account.
moderate_29.SQL: SELECT T1.account_id, T1.frequency FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T2.A3 = 'east Bohemia' AND STRFTIME('%Y', T1.date) BETWEEN '1995' AND '2000'

moderate_30.question: List the top nine districts, by descending order, from the highest to the lowest, the number of female account holders.
moderate_30.evidence: A2 refers to districts; Female refers to gender = 'F'
moderate_30.SQL: SELECT T2.A2, COUNT(T1.client_id) FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T1.gender = 'F' GROUP BY T2.district_id, T2.A2 ORDER BY COUNT(T1.client_id) DESC LIMIT 9

moderate_31.question: Which are the top ten withdrawals (non-credit card) by district names for the month of January 1996?
moderate_31.evidence: Non-credit card withdraws refers to type = 'VYDAJ'; January 1996 can be found by date LIKE '1996-01%' in the database; A2 means district names
moderate_31.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.A2 FROM district AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN trans AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T3.type = 'VYDAJ' AND T3.date LIKE '1996-01%' ORDER BY A2 ASC LIMIT 10

moderate_32.question: How many of the account holders in South Bohemia still do not own credit cards?
moderate_32.evidence: A3 contains the region names; South Bohemia is one of region names.
moderate_32.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T3.account_id) FROM district AS T1 INNER JOIN client AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN disp AS T3 ON T2.client_id = T3.client_id WHERE T1.A3 = 'south Bohemia' AND T3.type != 'OWNER'

moderate_33.question: Which district has highest active loan?
moderate_33.evidence: A3 refers to district names; Active loan refers to running contracts; Status = 'C' stands for running contract, OK so far; Status = 'D' stands for running contract, client in debt
moderate_33.SQL: SELECT T2.A3 FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN loan AS T3 ON T1.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T3.status IN ('C', 'D') GROUP BY T2.A3 ORDER BY SUM(T3.amount) DESC LIMIT 1

moderate_34.question: After making a credit card withdrawal, how many account/s with monthly issuance has a negative balance?
moderate_34.evidence: Negative balance means balance < 0; Operation = 'VYBER KARTOU' stands for credit card withdraw. Frequency = 'POPLATEK MESICNE' stands for monthly issurance
moderate_34.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.account_id) FROM trans AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE T1.balance < 0 AND T1.operation = 'VYBER KARTOU' AND T2.frequency = 'POPLATEK MESICNE'

moderate_35.question: Please list the reference names of the drivers who are eliminated in the first period in race number 20.
moderate_35.evidence: driver reference name refers to driverRef; first qualifying period refers to q1; drivers who are eliminated in the first qualifying period refers to 5 drivers with MAX(q1); race number refers to raceId;
moderate_35.SQL: SELECT T2.driverRef FROM qualifying AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T1.raceId = 20 ORDER BY T1.q1 DESC LIMIT 5

moderate_36.question: How many races in the year 2010 are held on grand prixs outside Asia and Europe?
moderate_36.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T3.raceId) FROM circuits AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T3 ON T3.circuitID = T1.circuitId WHERE T1.country NOT IN ( 'Bahrain', 'China', 'Singapore', 'Japan', 'Korea', 'Turkey', 'UAE', 'Malaysia', 'Spain', 'Monaco', 'Azerbaijan', 'Austria', 'Belgium', 'France', 'Germany', 'Hungary', 'Italy', 'UK' ) AND T3.year = 2010

moderate_37.question: For all the drivers who finished the game in race No. 592, who is the oldest?
moderate_37.evidence: drivers who finished the race refers to time is not empty (i.e. time IS NOT NULL); race number refers to raceId; date of birth refers to drivers.dob; The larger the birthday value, the younger the person is, and vice versa;
moderate_37.SQL: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname FROM drivers AS T1 INNER JOIN results AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T2.raceId = 592 AND T2.time IS NOT NULL AND T1.dob IS NOT NULL ORDER BY T1.dob ASC LIMIT 1

moderate_38.question: Who was the player that got the lap time of 0:01:27 in the race No. 161? Show his introduction website.
moderate_38.evidence: player and driver are synonyms; the lap time of 0:0M:SS refers to lapTime.time LIKE 'M:SS%';race number refers to raceId; introduction website of the drivers refers to url;
moderate_38.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.forename, T2.surname, T2.url FROM lapTimes AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T1.raceId = 161 AND T1.time LIKE '1:27%'

moderate_39.question: For the constructor which got the highest point in the race No. 9 , what is its introduction website?
moderate_39.evidence: race number refers to raceId; constructor which got the highest point refers to MAX(constructorResults.points); introduction website of the constructor refers to url;
moderate_39.SQL: SELECT T2.url FROM constructorResults AS T1 INNER JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T2.constructorId = T1.constructorId WHERE T1.raceId = 9 ORDER BY T1.points DESC LIMIT 1

moderate_40.question: For all the drivers who finished the game in race No. 872, who is the youngest?
moderate_40.evidence: race number refers to raceId; drivers who finished the race refers to time has value; the youngest is a driver where MAX(dob);
moderate_40.SQL: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname FROM drivers AS T1 INNER JOIN results AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T2.raceId = 872 AND T2.time IS NOT NULL ORDER BY T1.dob DESC LIMIT 1

moderate_41.question: For the driver who set the fastest lap speed, what is his nationality?
moderate_41.evidence: the fastest lap speed refers to (MAX) fastestLapSpeed;
moderate_41.SQL: SELECT T1.nationality FROM drivers AS T1 INNER JOIN results AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId ORDER BY T2.fastestLapSpeed DESC LIMIT 1

moderate_42.question: For the drivers who took part in the race in 1983/7/16, what's their race completion rate?
moderate_42.evidence: DIVIDE(COUNT(driverid when time has value ), (COUNT(driverid )) as percentage; in 1983/7/16 refers to when date = '1983-07-16'
moderate_42.SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.time IS NOT NULL THEN T2.driverId END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T2.driverId) FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN results AS T2 ON T2.raceId = T1.raceId WHERE T1.date = '1983-07-16'

moderate_43.question: List the names of all races that occurred in the earliest recorded year and month.
moderate_43.evidence: earliest recorded year and month refers to year = year(min(date)) and month = month(min(date));
moderate_43.SQL: SELECT name FROM races WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', date) = ( SELECT STRFTIME('%Y', date) FROM races ORDER BY date ASC LIMIT 1 ) AND STRFTIME('%m', date) = ( SELECT STRFTIME('%m', date) FROM races ORDER BY date ASC LIMIT 1 )

moderate_44.question: State the driver with the most points scored. Find his full name with that points.
moderate_44.evidence: the most points scored refers to max(points); Full name of the driver refers to drivers.forename and drivers.surname;
moderate_44.SQL: SELECT T3.forename, T3.surname, T2.points FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN driverStandings AS T2 ON T2.raceId = T1.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T3.driverId = T2.driverId ORDER BY T2.points DESC LIMIT 1

moderate_45.question: What is the best lap time recorded? List the driver and race with such recorded lap time.
moderate_45.evidence: the best lap time refers to min(milliseconds); List the driver refers to drivers.forename and drivers.surname; List the race refers to races.name
moderate_45.SQL: SELECT T2.milliseconds, T1.forename, T1.surname, T3.name FROM drivers AS T1 INNER JOIN lapTimes AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId INNER JOIN races AS T3 ON T2.raceId = T3.raceId ORDER BY T2.milliseconds ASC LIMIT 1

moderate_46.question: What is the average lap time for Lewis Hamilton in the 2009 Malaysian Grand Prix?
moderate_46.evidence: average lap time = AVG(milliseconds); 'Lewis Hamilton' refers to the full name of the driver; Full name of the driver refers to drivers.forename and drivers.surname; 'Malaysian Grand Prix' refers to races.name = 'Malaysian Grand Prix'
moderate_46.SQL: SELECT AVG(T2.milliseconds) FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN lapTimes AS T2 ON T2.raceId = T1.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T3.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T3.forename = 'Lewis' AND T3.surname = 'Hamilton' AND T1.year = 2009 AND T1.name = 'Malaysian Grand Prix'

moderate_47.question: Name the driver with the most winning. Mention his nationality and what is his maximum point scores.
moderate_47.evidence: Full name of the driver refers to drivers.forename and drivers.surname; the most winning refers to MAX(COUNT(wins)); average point scores refers to MAX(points);
moderate_47.SQL: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname, T1.nationality, MAX(T2.points) FROM drivers AS T1 INNER JOIN driverStandings AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T2.wins >= 1 GROUP BY T1.forename, T1.surname, T1.nationality ORDER BY COUNT(T2.wins) DESC LIMIT 1

moderate_48.question: List circuits which host 4 f1 races from year 1990 to 2000.
moderate_48.evidence: from year 1990 to 2000 refers to year(date) between 1990 and 2000;
moderate_48.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM circuits AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T2.circuitID = T1.circuitId WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T2.date) BETWEEN '1990' AND '2000' GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.raceId) = 4

moderate_49.question: How many times did Michael Schumacher won from races hosted in Sepang International Circuit?
moderate_49.evidence: win from races refers to max(points)
moderate_49.SQL: SELECT SUM(T2.wins) FROM drivers AS T1 INNER JOIN driverStandings AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId INNER JOIN races AS T3 ON T3.raceId = T2.raceId INNER JOIN circuits AS T4 ON T4.circuitId = T3.circuitId WHERE T1.forename = 'Michael' AND T1.surname = 'Schumacher' AND T4.name = 'Sepang International Circuit'

moderate_50.question: State the race and year of race in which Michael Schumacher had his fastest lap.
moderate_50.evidence: fastest lap refers to min(milliseconds); Alex Yoong refers to the full name of the driver; Full name of the driver refers to drivers.forename and drivers.surname;
moderate_50.SQL: SELECT T1.name, T1.year FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN lapTimes AS T2 ON T2.raceId = T1.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T3.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T3.forename = 'Michael' AND T3.surname = 'Schumacher' ORDER BY T2.milliseconds ASC LIMIT 1

moderate_51.question: Which was Lewis Hamilton first race? What was his points recorded for his first race event?
moderate_51.evidence: first race refers to min(Year); Lewis Hamiltonrefers to the full name of the driver; Full name of the driver refers to drivers.forename and drivers.surname;
moderate_51.SQL: SELECT T1.name, T2.points FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN driverStandings AS T2 ON T2.raceId = T1.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T3.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T3.forename = 'Lewis' AND T3.surname = 'Hamilton' ORDER BY T1.year ASC LIMIT 1

moderate_52.question: Calculate the average number of oxygen atoms in single-bonded molecules.
moderate_52.evidence: single-bonded molecules refers to bond_type = '-' ; average number of oxygen atom = AVG(element = 'o')
moderate_52.SQL: SELECT AVG(oxygen_count) FROM (SELECT T1.molecule_id, COUNT(T1.element) AS oxygen_count FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN bond AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id  WHERE T2.bond_type = '-' AND T1.element = 'o'  GROUP BY T1.molecule_id) AS oxygen_counts

moderate_53.question: What is the percentage of carbon in double-bond molecules?
moderate_53.evidence: carbon refers to element = 'c'; double-bond molecules refers to bond_type = '='; percentage = DIVIDE(SUM(element = 'c'), COUNT(atom_id))
moderate_53.SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN T1.element = 'c' THEN T1.atom_id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(DISTINCT T1.atom_id) FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN bond AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T2.bond_type = '='

moderate_54.question: Which type of label is the most numerous in atoms with hydrogen?
moderate_54.evidence: with hydrogen refers to element = 'h'; label most numerous in atoms refers to MAX(COUNT(label));
moderate_54.SQL: SELECT T.label FROM ( SELECT T2.label, COUNT(T2.molecule_id) FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T1.element = 'h' GROUP BY T2.label ORDER BY COUNT(T2.molecule_id) DESC LIMIT 1 ) t

moderate_55.question: What type of bond is there between the atoms TR004_8 and TR004_20?
moderate_55.evidence: type of bond refers to bond_type; between the atoms TR004_8 and TR004_20 refers to atom_id = 'TR004_8' AND atom_id2 = 'TR004_20' OR another way around
moderate_55.SQL: SELECT T1.bond_type FROM bond AS T1 INNER JOIN connected AS T2 ON T1.bond_id = T2.bond_id WHERE T2.atom_id = 'TR004_8' AND T2.atom_id2 = 'TR004_20' OR T2.atom_id2 = 'TR004_8' AND T2.atom_id = 'TR004_20'

moderate_56.question: What is the difference between the number of molecules that are carcinogenic and those that are not?
moderate_56.evidence: label = '+' means molecules are carcinogenic; label = '-' means molecules are non-carcinogenic; difference = SUBTRACT(SUM(label = '+'), SUM(label = '-'))
moderate_56.SQL: SELECT COUNT(CASE WHEN T.label = '+' THEN T.molecule_id ELSE NULL END) - COUNT(CASE WHEN T.label = '-' THEN T.molecule_id ELSE NULL END) AS diff_car_notcar FROM molecule t

moderate_57.question: What is the percentage of double bonds in the molecule TR008? Please provide your answer as a percentage with five decimal places.
moderate_57.evidence: double bond refers to bond_type = '='; TR008 is the molecule id; percentage = DIVIDE(SUM(bond_type = '='), COUNT(bond_id)) as percent where molecule_id = 'TR008'
moderate_57.SQL: SELECT ROUND(CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T.bond_type = '=' THEN T.bond_id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T.bond_id),5) FROM bond AS T WHERE T.molecule_id = 'TR008'

moderate_58.question: How much of the hydrogen in molecule TR206 is accounted for? Please provide your answer as a percentage with four decimal places.
moderate_58.evidence: hydrogen refers to element = 'h'; TR206 is the molecule id; percentage = DIVIDE(SUM(element = 'h'), COUNT(atom_id)) as percent where molecule_id = 'TR206'
moderate_58.SQL: SELECT ROUND(CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T.element = 'h' THEN T.atom_id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T.atom_id),4) FROM atom AS T WHERE T.molecule_id = 'TR206'

moderate_59.question: Please list top three molecules that have single bonds between two atoms and are not carcinogenic in alphabetical order.
moderate_59.evidence: label = '-' means molecules are not carcinogenic; single type bond refers to bond_type = '-'; list top three molecules refers to return molecule_id and order by molecule_id;
moderate_59.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.molecule_id FROM bond AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T1.bond_type = '-' AND T2.label = '-' ORDER BY T2.molecule_id LIMIT 3

moderate_60.question: How many bonds which involved atom 12 does molecule TR009 have?
moderate_60.evidence: TR009 is the molecule id;  involved atom 12 refers to atom_id = 'TR009_12' or atom_id2 = 'TR009_12'
moderate_60.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.bond_id) FROM bond AS T1 INNER JOIN connected AS T2 ON T1.bond_id = T2.bond_id WHERE T1.molecule_id = 'TR009' AND T2.atom_id = T1.molecule_id || '_1' AND T2.atom_id2 = T1.molecule_id || '_2'

moderate_61.question: What are the bond type and the atoms of the bond ID of TR001_6_9?
moderate_61.evidence: atoms refer to atom_id or atom_id2
moderate_61.SQL: SELECT T1.bond_type, T2.atom_id, T2.atom_id2 FROM bond AS T1 INNER JOIN connected AS T2 ON T1.bond_id = T2.bond_id WHERE T2.bond_id = 'TR001_6_9'

moderate_62.question: Which molecule does the atom TR001_10 belong to? Please state whether this molecule is carcinogenic or not.
moderate_62.evidence: TR001_10 is the atom id; label = '+' mean molecules are carcinogenic
moderate_62.SQL: SELECT T2.molecule_id , IIF(T2.label = '+', 'YES', 'NO') AS flag_carcinogenic FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T1.atom_id = 'TR001_10'

moderate_63.question: Among all the atoms from 21 to 25, list all the molecules that are carcinogenic.
moderate_63.evidence: atoms from 21 to 25 refers to SUBSTR(atom_id, 7, 2) between '21' and '25'; label = '+' mean molecules are carcinogenic
moderate_63.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.molecule_id FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE SUBSTR(T1.atom_id, -2) BETWEEN '21' AND '25' AND T2.label = '+'

moderate_64.question: What are the bonds that have phosphorus and nitrogen as their atom elements?
moderate_64.evidence: have phosphorus as atom elements refers to element = 'p'; have nitrogen as atom elements refers to element = 'n'
moderate_64.SQL: SELECT T2.bond_id FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN connected AS T2 ON T1.atom_id = T2.atom_id WHERE T2.bond_id IN ( SELECT T3.bond_id FROM connected AS T3 INNER JOIN atom AS T4 ON T3.atom_id = T4.atom_id WHERE T4.element = 'p' ) AND T1.element = 'n'

moderate_65.question: Is the molecule with the most double bonds carcinogenic?
moderate_65.evidence: double bond refers to bond_type = ' = '; label = '+' mean molecules are carcinogenic
moderate_65.SQL: SELECT T1.label FROM molecule AS T1 INNER JOIN ( SELECT T.molecule_id, COUNT(T.bond_type) FROM bond AS T WHERE T.bond_type = '=' GROUP BY T.molecule_id ORDER BY COUNT(T.bond_type) DESC LIMIT 1 ) AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id

challenging_1.question: Consider the average difference between K-12 enrollment and 15-17 enrollment of schools that are locally funded, list the names and DOC type of schools which has a difference above this average.
challenging_1.evidence: Difference between K-12 enrollment and 15-17 enrollment can be computed by `Enrollment (K-12)` - `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`
challenging_1.SQL: SELECT T2.School, T2.DOC FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.FundingType = 'Locally funded' AND (T1.`Enrollment (K-12)` - T1.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) > (SELECT AVG(T3.`Enrollment (K-12)` - T3.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) FROM frpm AS T3 INNER JOIN schools AS T4 ON T3.CDSCode = T4.CDSCode WHERE T4.FundingType = 'Locally funded')

challenging_2.question: Under whose administration is the school with the highest number of students scoring 1500 or more on the SAT? Indicate their full names.
challenging_2.evidence: full name means first name, last name; There are at most 3 administrators for each school; SAT Scores are greater or equal to 1500 refers to NumGE1500
challenging_2.SQL: SELECT T2.AdmFName1, T2.AdmLName1, T2.AdmFName2, T2.AdmLName2, T2.AdmFName3, T2.AdmLName3 FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode ORDER BY T1.NumGE1500 DESC LIMIT 1

challenging_3.question: What is the total number of non-chartered schools in the county of Los Angeles with a percent (%) of eligible free meals for grades 1 through 12 that is less than 0.18%?
challenging_3.evidence: non-chartered schools refer to schools whose Charter = 0; K-12 means grades 1 through 12; percent of eligible free rate for K-12 = `Free Meal Count (K-12)` * 100 / `Enrollment (K-12)`
challenging_3.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.School) FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.County = 'Los Angeles' AND T2.Charter = 0 AND CAST(T1.`Free Meal Count (K-12)` AS REAL) * 100 / T1.`Enrollment (K-12)` < 0.18

challenging_4.question: Of the schools that offers a magnet program serving a grade span of Kindergarten to 8th grade, how many offers Multiple Provision Types? List the number of cities that offers a Kindergarten to 8th grade span and indicate how many schools are there serving such grade span for each city.
challenging_4.evidence: Kindergarten to 8th grade refers to K-8; 'Offers a magnet program' means Magnet = 1; Multiple Provision Types refers to `NSLP Provision Status` = 'Multiple Provision Types'
challenging_4.SQL: SELECT T2.City, COUNT(T2.CDSCode) FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.Magnet = 1 AND T2.GSoffered = 'K-8' AND T1.`NSLP Provision Status` = 'Multiple Provision Types' GROUP BY T2.City

challenging_5.question: What are the valid e-mail addresses of the administrator of the school located in the San Bernardino county, City of San Bernardino City Unified that opened between 1/1/2009 to 12/31/2010 whose school types are public Intermediate/Middle Schools and Unified Schools?
challenging_5.evidence: Intermediate/Middle Schools refers to SOC = 62; Unified School refers to DOC = 54; years between 2009 and 2010 can refer to 'between 1/1/2009 to 12/31/2010'
challenging_5.SQL: SELECT T2.AdmEmail1, T2.AdmEmail2 FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.County = 'San Bernardino' AND T2.City = 'San Bernardino' AND T2.DOC = 54 AND strftime('%Y', T2.OpenDate) BETWEEN '2009' AND '2010' AND T2.SOC = 62

challenging_6.question: What was the difference in gas consumption between CZK-paying customers and EUR-paying customers in 2012?
challenging_6.evidence: Year 2012 can be presented as Between 201201 And 201212; The first 4 strings of the Date values in the yearmonth table can represent year; Difference in Consumption = CZK customers consumption in 2012 - EUR customers consumption in 2012
challenging_6.SQL: SELECT SUM(IIF(T1.Currency = 'CZK', T2.Consumption, 0)) - SUM(IIF(T1.Currency = 'EUR', T2.Consumption, 0)) FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE SUBSTR(T2.Date, 1, 4) = '2012'

challenging_7.question: What is the difference in the annual average consumption of the customers with the least amount of consumption paid in CZK for 2013 between SME and LAM, LAM and KAM, and KAM and SME?
challenging_7.evidence: annual average consumption of customer with the lowest consumption in each segment = total consumption per year / the number of customer with lowest consumption in each segment; Difference in annual average = SME's annual average - LAM's annual average; Difference in annual average = LAM's annual average - KAM's annual average; Year 2013 can be presented as Between 201301 And 201312; The first 4 strings of the Date values in the yearmonth table can represent year.
challenging_7.SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'SME', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.CustomerID) - CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'LAM', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.CustomerID) , CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'LAM', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.CustomerID) - CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'KAM', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.CustomerID) , CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'KAM', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.CustomerID) - CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'SME', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.CustomerID) FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T1.Currency = 'CZK' AND T2.Consumption = ( SELECT MIN(Consumption) FROM yearmonth ) AND T2.Date BETWEEN 201301 AND 201312

challenging_8.question: Which of the three segments—SME, LAM and KAM—has the biggest and lowest percentage increases in consumption paid in EUR between 2012 and 2013?
challenging_8.evidence: Increase or Decrease = consumption for 2013 - consumption for 2012; Percentage of Increase = (Increase or Decrease / consumption for 2013) * 100%; The first 4 strings of the Date values in the yearmonth table can represent year
challenging_8.SQL: SELECT CAST((SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'SME' AND T2.Date LIKE '2013%', T2.Consumption, 0)) - SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'SME' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0))) AS FLOAT) * 100 / SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'SME' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)), CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'LAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2013%', T2.Consumption, 0)) - SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'LAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS FLOAT) * 100 / SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'LAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)) , CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'KAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2013%', T2.Consumption, 0)) - SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'KAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS FLOAT) * 100 / SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'KAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)) FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID

challenging_9.question: For the customer who paid 634.8 in 2012/8/25, what was the consumption decrease rate from Year 2012 to 2013?
challenging_9.evidence: '2012/8/24' can be represented by '2012-08-24'; Consumption decrease rate = (consumption_2012 - consumption_2013) / consumption_2012
challenging_9.SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(SUBSTR(Date, 1, 4) = '2012', Consumption, 0)) - SUM(IIF(SUBSTR(Date, 1, 4) = '2013', Consumption, 0)) AS FLOAT) / SUM(IIF(SUBSTR(Date, 1, 4) = '2012', Consumption, 0)) FROM yearmonth WHERE CustomerID = ( SELECT T1.CustomerID FROM transactions_1k AS T1 INNER JOIN gasstations AS T2 ON T1.GasStationID = T2.GasStationID WHERE T1.Date = '2012-08-25' AND T1.Price = 634.8 )

challenging_10.question: List out the account numbers of female clients who are oldest and has lowest average salary, calculate the gap between this lowest average salary with the highest average salary?
challenging_10.evidence: Female means gender = 'F'; A11 refers to average salary; Gap = highest average salary - lowest average salary; If the person A's birthdate > B's birthdate, it means that person B is order than person A.
challenging_10.SQL: SELECT T1.account_id , ( SELECT MAX(A11) - MIN(A11) FROM district ) FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN disp AS T3 ON T1.account_id = T3.account_id INNER JOIN client AS T4 ON T3.client_id = T4.client_id WHERE T2.district_id = ( SELECT district_id FROM client WHERE gender = 'F' ORDER BY birth_date ASC LIMIT 1 ) ORDER BY T2.A11 DESC LIMIT 1

challenging_11.question: For the branch which located in the south Bohemia with biggest number of inhabitants, what is the percentage of the male clients?
challenging_11.evidence: Percentage of the male clients = DIVIDE(COUNT(male clients), COUNT(clients)) * 100; Male refers to gender = 'M', A3 is the region name. A4 contains the information about inhabitants.
challenging_11.SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(T1.gender = 'M') AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.client_id) FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T2.A3 = 'south Bohemia' GROUP BY T2.A4 ORDER BY T2.A4 DESC LIMIT 1

challenging_12.question: For the client whose loan was approved first in 1993/7/5, what is the increase rate of his/her account balance from 1993/3/22 to 1998/12/27?
challenging_12.evidence: Increase rate of his/her account balance = [(balance of date A - balance of date B) / balance of Date B] * 100%
challenging_12.SQL: SELECT CAST((SUM(IIF(T3.date = '1998-12-27', T3.balance, 0)) - SUM(IIF(T3.date = '1993-03-22', T3.balance, 0))) AS REAL) * 100 / SUM(IIF(T3.date = '1993-03-22', T3.balance, 0)) FROM loan AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN trans AS T3 ON T3.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE T1.date = '1993-07-05'

challenging_13.question: For loans contracts which are still running where client are in debt, list the district of the and the state the percentage unemployment rate increment from year 1995 to 1996.
challenging_13.evidence: Unemployment increment rate in percentage = [(unemployment rate 2016 - unemployment rate 2015) / unemployment rate 2015] * 100; unemployment rate 2015 appears in the A12; unemployment rate 2016 appears in the A13; Loan contracts which are still running where client are in debt can be presented as status = 'D'
challenging_13.SQL: SELECT CAST((T3.A13 - T3.A12) AS REAL) * 100 / T3.A12 FROM loan AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN district AS T3 ON T2.district_id = T3.district_id WHERE T1.status = 'D'

challenging_14.question: Please list the account types that are not eligible for loans, and the average income of residents in the district where the account is located exceeds $8000 but is no more than $9000.
challenging_14.evidence: A11 represents the average salary; Salary and income share the similar meanings; when the account type = 'OWNER', it's eligible for loans
challenging_14.SQL: SELECT T3.type FROM district AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN disp AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T3.type != 'OWNER' AND T1.A11 BETWEEN 8000 AND 9000

challenging_15.question: What was the growth rate of the total amount of loans across all accounts for a male client between 1996 and 1997?
challenging_15.evidence: Growth rate = (sum of amount_1997 - sum of amount_1996) / (sum of amount_1996) * 100%; Male refers to gender = 'M'
challenging_15.SQL: SELECT CAST((SUM(CASE WHEN STRFTIME('%Y', T1.date) = '1997' THEN T1.amount ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN STRFTIME('%Y', T1.date) = '1996' THEN T1.amount ELSE 0 END)) AS REAL) * 100 / SUM(CASE WHEN STRFTIME('%Y', T1.date) = '1996' THEN T1.amount ELSE 0 END) FROM loan AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN disp AS T3 ON T3.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN client AS T4 ON T4.client_id = T3.client_id WHERE T4.gender = 'M' AND T3.type = 'OWNER'

challenging_16.question: Paul di Resta was in the No. 853 race, what percent faster did he finish in the 853rd race than the next race for the fastest lap speed?
challenging_16.evidence: Paul di Resta refers to the full name of the driver; Full name of the driver refers to drivers.forename ='Paul' and drivers.surname = 'di Resta'; race number refers to raceId; percentage = DIVIDE(SUBTRACT(fastestLapSpeed(raceId = 853), (fastestLapSpeed (raceId = 854)) * 100 , (fastestLapSpeed(raceId = 853))
challenging_16.SQL: SELECT (SUM(IIF(T2.raceId = 853, T2.fastestLapSpeed, 0)) - SUM(IIF(T2.raceId = 854, T2.fastestLapSpeed, 0))) * 100 / SUM(IIF(T2.raceId = 853, T2.fastestLapSpeed, 0)) FROM drivers AS T1 INNER JOIN results AS T2 ON T2.driverId = T1.driverId WHERE T1.forename = 'Paul' AND T1.surname = 'di Resta'

challenging_17.question: Calculate the percentage whereby Hamilton was not at the 1st track of the the f1 circuit since 2010.
challenging_17.evidence: percentage = DIVIDE(COUNT(raceId) where surname = 'Hamilton' and position>1), (COUNT(raceId) where surname = 'Hamilton'); since 2010 refers to year >= 2010
challenging_17.SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.position <> 1 THEN T2.position END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T2.driverStandingsId) FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN driverStandings AS T2 ON T2.raceId = T1.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T3.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T3.surname = 'Hamilton' AND T1.year >= 2010

challenging_18.question: How much faster in percentage is the champion than the driver who finished the race last in the 2008 Australian Grand Prix?
challenging_18.evidence: how much faster in percentage = divide(subtract(incremental time, champion time), last_driver time) * 100; last driver finished time = incremental time + champion time; only champion's finished time is represented by 'HH:MM:SS.mmm'; finished the game refers to time is not null
challenging_18.SQL: WITH time_in_seconds AS ( SELECT T1.positionOrder, CASE WHEN T1.positionOrder = 1 THEN (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 1, 1) AS REAL) * 3600) + (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 3, 2) AS REAL) * 60) + CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 6) AS REAL) ELSE CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 2) AS REAL) END AS time_seconds FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId WHERE T2.name = 'Australian Grand Prix' AND T1.time IS NOT NULL AND T2.year = 2008 ), champion_time AS ( SELECT time_seconds FROM time_in_seconds WHERE positionOrder = 1), last_driver_incremental AS ( SELECT time_seconds FROM time_in_seconds WHERE positionOrder = (SELECT MAX(positionOrder) FROM time_in_seconds) ) SELECT (CAST((SELECT time_seconds FROM last_driver_incremental) AS REAL) * 100) / (SELECT time_seconds + (SELECT time_seconds FROM last_driver_incremental) FROM champion_time)

challenging_19.question: Please calculate the race completion percentage of Japanese drivers from 2007 to 2009.
challenging_19.evidence: from 2007 to 2009 refers to year between 2007 and 2009; race completion refers to time is not null; percentage = Divide(COUNT(DriverID where time is not null and year between 2007 and 2009),Count (DriverID where year between 2007 and 2009))*100;
challenging_19.SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.time IS NOT NULL, 1, 0)) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.raceId) FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 on T1.raceId = T2.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 on T1.driverId = T3.driverId WHERE T3.nationality = 'Japanese' AND T2.year BETWEEN 2007 AND 2009

challenging_20.question: What is the average time in seconds of champion for each year, before year 1975?
challenging_20.evidence: only champion's finished time is represented by 'HH:MM:SS.mmm'; finished the game refers to time is not null; before year 1975 refers to year < 1975;
challenging_20.SQL: WITH time_in_seconds AS ( SELECT T2.year, T2.raceId, T1.positionOrder, CASE WHEN T1.positionOrder = 1 THEN (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 1, 1) AS REAL) * 3600) + (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 3, 2) AS REAL) * 60) + CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 6,2) AS REAL )   + CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 9) AS REAL)/1000 ELSE 0 END AS time_seconds FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId WHERE T1.time IS NOT NULL ), champion_time AS ( SELECT year, raceId, time_seconds FROM time_in_seconds WHERE positionOrder = 1 ) SELECT year, AVG(time_seconds) FROM champion_time WHERE year < 1975 GROUP BY year HAVING AVG(time_seconds) IS NOT NULL

challenging_21.question: From 2000 to 2005, what percentage of drivers who were born before 1985 and the lap numbers were over 50?
challenging_21.evidence: born before 1985 refers to year(dob)<1985; in 2000 to 2005 refers to year between 2000 and 2005; percentage = Divide(COUNT(driverId where year (dob) <1985 and laps >50),COUNT(DriverID where year between 2000 and 2005) *100;
challenging_21.SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(STRFTIME('%Y', T3.dob) < '1985' AND T1.laps > 50, 1, 0)) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*) FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 on T1.raceId = T2.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 on T1.driverId = T3.driverId WHERE T2.year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2005

challenging_22.question: List down top 3 German drivers who has the shortest average pit stop duration and were born between 1980-1985.
challenging_22.evidence: Full name of the driver refers to drivers.forename and drivers.surname; born between 1980-1985 refers to 1980< year(dob)>1985; Average pitstop duration refers to Divide(SUM(duration),COUNT(duration)); shortest average refers to Min(avg(duration));
challenging_22.SQL: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM pitStops AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 on T1.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T2.nationality = 'German' AND STRFTIME('%Y', T2.dob) BETWEEN '1980' AND '1985' GROUP BY T2.forename, T2.surname ORDER BY AVG(T1.duration) LIMIT 3

challenging_23.question: What is the constructor reference name of the champion in the 2009 Singapore Grand Prix? Please give its website.
challenging_23.evidence: the time of the champion shows in the format of "minutes: seconds.millionsecond" in which Max(time); constructor reference name refers to constructorRef; website refers to url
challenging_23.SQL: SELECT T3.constructorRef, T3.url FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 on T1.raceId = T2.raceId INNER JOIN constructors AS T3 on T1.constructorId = T3.constructorId WHERE T2.name = 'Singapore Grand Prix' AND T2.year = 2009 AND T1.time LIKE '_:%:__.___'

challenging_24.question: Which constructor scored most points from Monaco Grand Prix between 1980 and 2010? List the score, name and nationality of this team.
challenging_24.evidence: Monaco Grand Priz refers to the race; race in year between 1980 and 2010
challenging_24.SQL: SELECT SUM(T1.points), T2.name, T2.nationality FROM constructorResults AS T1 INNER JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId INNER JOIN races AS T3 ON T3.raceid = T1.raceid WHERE T3.name = 'Monaco Grand Prix' AND T3.year BETWEEN 1980 AND 2010 GROUP BY T2.name ORDER BY SUM(T1.points) DESC LIMIT 1

challenging_25.question: What is full name of the racer who ranked 1st in the 3rd qualifying race held in the Marina Bay Street Circuit in 2008?
challenging_25.evidence: Ranked 1st in the 3rd qualifying race refer to MIN(q3); 2008 is the year of race; full name of racer = forename, surname
challenging_25.SQL: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM qualifying AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 on T1.driverId = T2.driverId INNER JOIN races AS T3 ON T1.raceid = T3.raceid WHERE q3 IS NOT NULL AND T3.year = 2008 AND T3.circuitId IN ( SELECT circuitId FROM circuits WHERE name = 'Marina Bay Street Circuit' ) ORDER BY CAST(SUBSTR(q3, 1, INSTR(q3, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER) * 60 + CAST(SUBSTR(q3, INSTR(q3, ':') + 1, INSTR(q3, '.') - INSTR(q3, ':') - 1) AS REAL) + CAST(SUBSTR(q3, INSTR(q3, '.') + 1) AS REAL) / 1000 ASC LIMIT 1

challenging_26.question: Among all the lap records set on various circuits, what is the time for the fastest one?
challenging_26.SQL: SELECT time FROM lapTimes ORDER BY (CASE WHEN INSTR(time, ':') <> INSTR(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1), ':') + INSTR(time, ':') THEN CAST(SUBSTR(time, 1, INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL) * 3600 ELSE 0 END) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') - 2 * (INSTR(time, ':') = INSTR(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1), ':') + INSTR(time, ':')), INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL) * 60) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1, INSTR(time, '.') - INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL)) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, '.') + 1) AS REAL) / 1000) ASC LIMIT 1

challenging_27.question: Which top 20 driver created the shortest lap time ever record in a Formula_1 race? Please give them full names.
challenging_27.evidence: shortest lap time refers to MIN(time); the time format for the shortest lap time is 'MM:SS.mmm' or 'M:SS.mmm'; full name of the driver refers to forename, surname
challenging_27.SQL: WITH lap_times_in_seconds AS (SELECT driverId, (CASE WHEN SUBSTR(time, 1, INSTR(time, ':') - 1) <> '' THEN CAST(SUBSTR(time, 1, INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL) * 60 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1, INSTR(time, '.') - INSTR(time, ':') - 1) <> '' THEN CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1, INSTR(time, '.') - INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL) ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, '.') + 1) <> '' THEN CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, '.') + 1) AS REAL) / 1000 ELSE 0 END) AS time_in_seconds FROM lapTimes) SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, T1.driverId FROM (SELECT driverId, MIN(time_in_seconds) AS min_time_in_seconds FROM lap_times_in_seconds GROUP BY driverId) AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId ORDER BY T1.min_time_in_seconds ASC LIMIT 20

challenging_28.question: On average how many carcinogenic molecules are single bonded?
challenging_28.evidence: carcinogenic molecules refers to label = '+'; single-bonded refers to bond_type = '-'; average = DIVIDE(SUM(bond_type = '-'), COUNT(atom_id))
challenging_28.SQL: SELECT AVG(single_bond_count) FROM (SELECT T3.molecule_id, COUNT(T1.bond_type) AS single_bond_count FROM bond AS T1  INNER JOIN atom AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id INNER JOIN molecule AS T3 ON T3.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T1.bond_type = '-' AND T3.label = '+' GROUP BY T3.molecule_id) AS subquery

challenging_29.question: What elements are in the TR004_8_9 bond atoms?
challenging_29.evidence: TR004_8_9 bond atoms refers to bond_id = 'TR004_8_9';
