challenging_29.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.element FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN connected AS T2 ON T1.atom_id = T2.atom_id WHERE T2.bond_id = 'TR004_8_9'

challenging_30.question: What elements are in a double type bond?
challenging_30.evidence: double type bond refers to bond_type = '=';
challenging_30.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.element FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN bond AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id INNER JOIN connected AS T3 ON T1.atom_id = T3.atom_id WHERE T2.bond_type = '='

challenging_31.question: Which element is the least numerous in non-carcinogenic molecules?
challenging_31.evidence: label = '-' means molecules are non-carcinogenic; least numerous refers to MIN(COUNT(element));
challenging_31.SQL: SELECT T.element FROM (SELECT T1.element, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.molecule_id) FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T2.label = '-' GROUP BY T1.element ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T1.molecule_id) ASC LIMIT 1) t

challenging_32.question: How many atoms with iodine and with sulfur type elements are there in single bond molecules?
challenging_32.evidence: with iodine element refer to element = 'i'; with sulfur element refers to element = 's'; single type bond refers to bond_type = '-'; Should consider the distinct atoms when counting;
challenging_32.SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN T1.element = 'i' THEN T1.atom_id ELSE NULL END) AS iodine_nums , COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN T1.element = 's' THEN T1.atom_id ELSE NULL END) AS sulfur_nums FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN connected AS T2 ON T1.atom_id = T2.atom_id INNER JOIN bond AS T3 ON T2.bond_id = T3.bond_id WHERE T3.bond_type = '-'

challenging_33.question: What percentage of carcinogenic-type molecules does not contain fluorine?
challenging_33.evidence: label = '+' mean molecules are carcinogenic; contain fluorine refers to element = 'f'; percentage = DIVIDE(SUM(element = 'f') * 100, COUNT(molecule_id)) where label = '+'; Should consider the distinct atoms when counting;
challenging_33.SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN T1.element <> 'f' THEN T2.molecule_id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(DISTINCT T2.molecule_id) FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T2.label = '+'

challenging_34.question: What is the percentage of carcinogenic molecules in triple type bonds?
challenging_34.evidence: label = '+' mean molecules are carcinogenic; triple bond refers to bond_type = '#'; percentage = DIVIDE(SUM(bond_type = '#') * 100, COUNT(bond_id)) as percent where label = '+'
challenging_34.SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN T2.label = '+' THEN T2.molecule_id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(DISTINCT T2.molecule_id) FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id INNER JOIN bond AS T3 ON T2.molecule_id = T3.molecule_id WHERE T3.bond_type = '#'

challenging_35.question: Please list top three elements of the toxicology of the molecule TR000 in alphabetical order.
challenging_35.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T.element FROM atom AS T WHERE T.molecule_id = 'TR000' ORDER BY T.element LIMIT 3

challenging_37.question: Which bond type accounted for the majority of the bonds found in molecule TR010 and state whether or not this molecule is carcinogenic?
challenging_37.evidence: TR010 is the molecule id; majority of the bond found refers to MAX(COUNT(bond_type));
challenging_37.SQL: SELECT T.bond_type FROM ( SELECT T1.bond_type, COUNT(T1.molecule_id) FROM bond AS T1  WHERE T1.molecule_id = 'TR010' GROUP BY T1.bond_type ORDER BY COUNT(T1.molecule_id) DESC LIMIT 1 ) AS T

challenging_39.question: List all the elements of atoms that can not bond with any other atoms.

challenging_39.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T.element FROM atom AS T WHERE T.element NOT IN ( SELECT DISTINCT T1.element FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN connected AS T2 ON T1.atom_id = T2.atom_id )

challenging_40.SQL: SELECT T2.element FROM connected AS T1 INNER JOIN atom AS T2 ON T1.atom_id = T2.atom_id WHERE T1.bond_id = 'TR144_8_19'

challenging_41.question: List the elements of all the triple bonds.
challenging_41.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T3.element FROM bond AS T1 INNER JOIN connected AS T2 ON T1.bond_id = T2.bond_id INNER JOIN atom AS T3 ON T2.atom_id = T3.atom_id WHERE T1.bond_type = '#'

challenging_42.question: What is the composition of element chlorine in percentage among the single bond molecules?
challenging_42.evidence: element chlorine refers to element = 'cl'; single bond refers to bond_type = '-'; percentage = DIVIDE(SUM(element = 'cl'), COUNT(atom_id)) as percent where bond_type = '-'
challenging_42.SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T.element = 'cl' THEN T.atom_id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T.atom_id) FROM ( SELECT T1.atom_id, T1.element FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id INNER JOIN bond AS T3 ON T2.molecule_id = T3.molecule_id WHERE T3.bond_type = '-' ) AS T

challenging_45.question: Tally the toxicology element of the 4th atom of each molecule that was carcinogenic.

challenging_45.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.element FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T2.label = '+' AND SUBSTR(T1.atom_id, -1) = '4' AND LENGTH(T1.atom_id) = 7

challenging_46.question: What is the ratio of Hydrogen elements in molecule ID TR006? List the ratio with its label.
challenging_46.evidence: hydrogen refers to element = 'h'; ratio = DIVIDE(SUM(element = 'h'), count(element)) where molecule_id = 'TR006' ; label = '+' mean molecules are carcinogenic; label = '-' means molecules are non-carcinogenic
challenging_46.SQL: WITH SubQuery AS (SELECT DISTINCT T1.atom_id, T1.element, T1.molecule_id, T2.label FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T2.molecule_id = 'TR006') SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN element = 'h' THEN atom_id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) / (CASE WHEN COUNT(atom_id) = 0 THEN NULL ELSE COUNT(atom_id) END) AS ratio, label FROM SubQuery GROUP BY label

challenging_47.SQL: SELECT T1.element FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN connected AS T2 ON T1.atom_id = T2.atom_id INNER JOIN bond AS T3 ON T2.bond_id = T3.bond_id WHERE T3.bond_id = 'TR001_10_11'
